mechanism_narrative: A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. The effect is continuous and does not require a specific threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend slope and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns following declining-rate periods are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration analogous to the Sahm rule, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as earnings expectations are revised downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to outperform an unconditional baseline strategy over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS accelerating) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL rising above trend) jointly signal an overheating economy that raises the probability of aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone is sufficient; the interaction of tight labor markets with persistent inflation is required to generate the signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 4%
failure_condition: Joint trigger condition produces forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional mean (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, or single-series models achieve equal or better Rank IC than the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST housing starts signal robust consumer demand and construction-sector momentum that feeds through to broader economic growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns, but only when the economy is in an expansionary demand regime as confirmed by positive GDPC1 growth; in contractionary regimes the same signal loses predictive power or reverses.
regime_hint: demand expansion regime with positive GDPC1 growth
failure_condition: The positive relationship between HOUST trend and forward returns fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically within periods of positive GDPC1 growth, or the sign of the effect flips within the expansion regime across two consecutive market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in RSAFS retail sales reflects strengthening consumer spending, a leading indicator of corporate revenue growth, producing modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as market participants incrementally price in improved near-term earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS trend readings show Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no meaningful short-horizon predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in INDPRO exceeding 1% signals contracting industrial output consistent with an emerging supply-side recession, which precedes broad earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO declines are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) across a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is simultaneously decelerating on a YoY basis, markets price in an approaching end to the tightening cycle, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as discount rate expectations ease. This effect requires the joint condition of restrictive policy plus falling inflation; high rates alone with persistent inflation do not trigger it.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY change decelerating for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns under the joint trigger are not significantly different from baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect only holds when tested with FEDFUNDS alone, indicating no true interaction benefit.

mechanism_narrative: Steady payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) sustained over multiple months signals durable late-cycle expansion strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but this relationship weakens or reverses once the expansion matures into a labor-market overheating phase associated with imminent policy tightening.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trend and 60-day forward returns drops below 0.03 or turns negative specifically during late-cycle expansion windows identified by low and falling UNRATE alongside rising FEDFUNDS.
